title: Jenkins Agent Offline Alerts
app: Splunk App for Jenkins, Jenkins API
spl:
index=cicd sourcetype="jenkins:computer" (offline="true" OR offline=true)
| lookup jenkins_agent_tier displayName OUTPUT tier
| where tier="production"
| table _time, displayName, offline, labels, numExecutors
| sort -_time
description: Operational paging when executors disappearтАФcomplements UC-12.2.10 with severity tiers and agent labels (prod vs dev).
value: Operational paging when executors disappearтАФcomplements UC-12.2.10 with severity tiers and agent labels (prod vs dev).
grandmaExplanation: Operational paging when executors disappear тАФ complements UC-12.2.10 with severity tiers and agent labels (prod vs dev) тАФ so code changes that fail, leak secrets, or skip checks are caught early.
implementation: Tag agents in lookup. Page immediately for production-labeled offline agents. Auto-create incident if >30% of pool offline.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
тАв Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk App for Jenkins, Jenkins API.
тАв Ensure the following data sources are available: `/computer/api/json` `offline=true`, agent heartbeat.
тАв For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 тАФ Configure data collection
Tag agents in lookup. Page immediately for production-labeled offline agents. Auto-create incident if >30% of pool offline.

Step 2 тАФ Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype="jenkins:computer" (offline="true" OR offline=true)
| lookup jenkins_agent_tier displayName OUTPUT tier
| where tier="production"
| table _time, displayName, offline, labels, numExecutors
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**Jenkins Agent Offline Alerts** тАФ Operational paging when executors disappearтАФcomplements UC-12.2.10 with severity tiers and agent labels (prod vs dev).

Documented **Data sources**: `/computer/api/json` `offline=true`, agent heartbeat. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk App for Jenkins, Jenkins API. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedтАФrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cicd; **sourcetype**: jenkins:computer. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

тАв Scopes the data: index=cicd, sourcetype="jenkins:computer". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
тАв Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
тАв Filters the current rows with `where tier="production"` тАФ typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
тАв Pipeline stage (see **Jenkins Agent Offline Alerts**): table _time, displayName, offline, labels, numExecutors
тАв Orders rows with `sort` тАФ combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 тАФ Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 тАФ Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (offline prod agents), Single value (count), Status grid (agent ├Ч tier).
visualization: Table (offline prod agents), Single value (count), Status grid (agent ├Ч tier).
